notes: Routing-first evals. Each prompt should cause Claude to load the auth0-custom-domains skill and pick the correct capability. Assertions also check that behavioral guardrails from SKILL.md are respected (e.g. correct type value, GET-merge-PATCH for metadata, no speculative create probe).

evals.prompt: Check the health of my Auth0 custom domains.
evals.expected_output: Should route to capability 5 (Check domain health). Read-only; lists domains, compares DNS to expected CNAME, probes reachability + TLS, flags renewal-at-risk if DNS was changed after verification.
assertions: Invokes the auth0-custom-domains skill
Routes to capability 5 (Check domain health)
Surfaces the active Auth0 CLI tenant before fetching state
Uses auth0 api get custom-domains (or equivalent) to list domains
Compares live DNS via dig against verification.methods[0].record
Probes HTTPS reachability (curl) and/or TLS cert expiry
Does NOT perform any writes (no create/PATCH/delete)
Does NOT attempt a speculative create to probe credit-card-on-file status

evals.prompt: Set up login.example.com as a custom domain on my Auth0 tenant. My DNS is at Cloudflare.
evals.expected_output: Should route to capability 1 (Set up a custom domain). Gathers inputs one at a time (hostname, then confirms tenant), creates the domain with type: auth0_managed_certs, detects Cloudflare via NS lookup, routes to Tier 1 via Cloudflare MCP, writes the CNAME with proxied=false, triggers verification, polls until ready.
assertions: Invokes the auth0-custom-domains skill
Routes to capability 1 (Set up a custom domain)
Gathers inputs as plain conversational text, not a structured widget
Uses type: auth0_managed_certs (short form, NOT auth0_managed_certificates)
Runs dig +short NS on the root domain to detect the provider
Routes to Tier 1 Cloudflare and uses the Cloudflare MCP
Sets proxied: false on the CNAME record
For a subdomain, speculatively includes relying_party_identifier (eTLD+1) and retries without it on operation_not_supported
Triggers verify after DNS propagates
Polls GET /custom-domains/{id} with backoff until status becomes ready
Does NOT suggest a plan upgrade on 403 (directs to Billing for credit-card-on-file instead)

evals.prompt: My custom domain login.acme.com has been stuck in pending_verification for over an hour.
evals.expected_output: Should route to capability 2 (Troubleshoot verification). Walks the diagnostic ladder: fetch current state, compare DNS to expected, check for proxy/flattening/conflicting records/propagation/private-zone, retry verification. Does NOT delete and recreate.
assertions: Invokes the auth0-custom-domains skill
Routes to capability 2 (Troubleshoot verification)
Surfaces the active tenant before reading domain state
Fetches the domain's current state and expected verification record
Walks the diagnostic ladder in order (DNS compare, proxy, flattening, conflicting records, propagation)
Uses dig against an external resolver (8.8.8.8 or 1.1.1.1) to cross-check propagation
Does NOT suggest deleting and recreating the domain as a first-line fix
Does NOT retry verification in a tight loop

evals.prompt: I have three custom domains on this tenant. Make login-eu.example.com the default, and set the relying party identifier on login.example.com to example.com.
evals.expected_output: Should route to capability 3 (Manage existing domains). Lists current state, stages both changes as a batch, confirms tenant, shows diffs, applies PATCH /tenants/settings for the default and PATCH /custom-domains/{id} for the RPID. Warns that existing passkeys become invalid when RPID changes.
assertions: Invokes the auth0-custom-domains skill
Routes to capability 3 (Manage existing domains)
Fetches current custom domains and tenant settings once, reuses cached state
Confirms the active tenant before PATCHing
Uses PATCH /custom-domains/default or PATCH /tenants/settings for the default (does NOT reference a non-existent `auth0 domains default set` CLI command)
Uses PATCH /custom-domains/{id} with relying_party_identifier for the RPID change
Shows a diff (current → new) before applying each change
Warns that changing relying_party_identifier invalidates existing passkeys
Does NOT attempt to change certificate type via PATCH

evals.prompt: Tag login.example.com with region=us-east and brand=acme so Actions can read it.
evals.expected_output: Should route to capability 3 (Manage existing domains) and use the GET-merge-PATCH pattern for domain_metadata. Confirms the tenant, reads current metadata, merges the new keys client-side, PATCHes the full merged object, enforces the 10-key / 255-char limits.
assertions: Invokes the auth0-custom-domains skill
Routes to capability 3 and specifically the domain_metadata operation
GETs current domain_metadata before writing
Merges the new keys into the existing object client-side (GET-merge-PATCH)
PATCHes the full merged object, not just the delta
Enforces the 10-key total limit and 255-char key/value limits before the write
Mentions that Actions reads event.custom_domain.domain_metadata
Does NOT claim that setting a value to null is the way to remove keys (should instead omit the key from the merged object)

evals.prompt: Remove login-legacy.example.com from my Auth0 tenant. DNS is at Route 53.
evals.expected_output: Should route to capability 4 (Remove a custom domain). Runs the default-safe flow: tenant confirm, default-domain check, only-domain check, then shows the full list of places the domain could be referenced and asks the user to pick skip / quick scan / deep scan before scanning anything. After the user's decision (or on skip), confirms explicitly and deletes from Auth0, then cleans up the CNAME in Route 53 using the exact-match DELETE pattern.
assertions: Invokes the auth0-custom-domains skill
Routes to capability 4 (Remove a custom domain)
Confirms the active tenant before deleting
Checks whether the domain is the default (reads default_custom_domain_id)
Checks whether it is the only custom domain on the tenant
Shows the user the full list of surfaces the domain could be referenced on (API-reachable and external) before scanning anything
Asks the user to choose: skip the scan, run a quick scan, or run a deep scan (does NOT scan by default)
If the user chooses quick scan, checks clients + tenant settings + emails/provider + email templates (from and body) in parallel
If the user chooses deep scan, extends to actions, rules, hooks, branding templates, organizations, connections, resource servers, log streams, prompts

After any scan, reminds the user that external surfaces (SDK configs, backend JWT validators, SAML SP metadata, IaC, third-party integrations, email DNS SPF/DKIM/DMARC) are not reachable and must be hand-checked
Requires explicit yes confirmation that shows the full impact and reflects the scan tier chosen
Uses DELETE /custom-domains/{id} for the Auth0 side
Detects Route 53 via NS and attempts automated CNAME cleanup, not manual
For the Route 53 DELETE, first reads the live record exact values (Name, Type, TTL, Value) then submits an exact-match change batch

evals.prompt: Something's wrong with my Auth0 custom domain, can you look at it?
evals.expected_output: Ambiguous intent. Should default-route to capability 5 (Check domain health) as the safe, read-only starter, surface findings, then recommend a follow-up capability (usually Troubleshoot verification).
assertions: Invokes the auth0-custom-domains skill
Routes to capability 5 (Check domain health) first, not directly to Troubleshoot
Does NOT perform any writes before the user has seen the health report
After the health report, recommends the appropriate follow-up capability based on findings

evals.prompt: Create a custom domain login.example.com with self-managed certs on my Free tenant.
evals.expected_output: Should route to capability 1 but surface two blockers up front: self-managed certs require Enterprise, and Free tenants need a credit card on file. Should NOT silently attempt the create and fail with a confusing 403.
assertions: Invokes the auth0-custom-domains skill
Routes to capability 1 (Set up a custom domain)
Uses type: self_managed_certs in the create body (short form)
Surfaces the Enterprise requirement for self_managed_certs before attempting the create
Surfaces the Free-tier credit-card-on-file requirement before attempting the create
Directs the user to Dashboard → Tenant Settings → Billing for the card (does NOT suggest a plan upgrade to fix a 403 on Free)

evals.prompt: I want to switch login.example.com from Auth0-managed to self-managed certs.
evals.expected_output: Should route to capability 3, explain that type is not PATCHable, and outline the delete-and-recreate path with a warning about auth downtime. Does NOT attempt PATCH with type in the body.
assertions: Invokes the auth0-custom-domains skill
Routes to capability 3 (Manage existing domains)
Explains that type is fixed at create time and rejected by PATCH
Routes the user to delete + recreate (capability 4 then capability 1)
Warns about auth downtime during the DNS + reverse-proxy cutover
Does NOT send type in a PATCH body
